back_translation: When Isaac was old and his eyes had become so dim that he could no longer see, he called Esau his older son and said to him, "My son." Esau answered, "Here I am."
Isaac said, "Behold, I am now old, and I do not know when the day of my death will come.
Now take your weapons out into the field — your quiver and your bow — and hunt some game for me.
Then prepare a tasty dish that I love and bring it to me to eat, so that my heart may bless you before I die."
But Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to Esau his son. So when Esau went out into the field to hunt some game and bring it back,
Rebekah said to Jacob her son, "Behold, I overheard your father saying to Esau your brother,
'Bring me some game and prepare for me a tasty dish to eat, so that I may bless you before the LORD before I die.'
Now then, my son, listen to my voice and do as I am instructing you.
Go to the flock and bring me two good young male goats, and I will prepare them as a tasty dish for your father, the way he loves.
You shall bring it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before he dies."
Jacob answered Rebekah his mother, "Look, Esau my brother is a hairy man, but I am a smooth-skinned man.
What if my father feels me? Then I shall be in his eyes as a deceiver, and I shall bring upon myself a curse rather than a blessing."
His mother said to him, "My son, let your curse be upon me. Just listen to my voice and go get them for me."
So Jacob went and got them and brought them to his mother, and his mother prepared a tasty dish the way his father loved.
Then Rebekah took the finest clothes of Esau her older son, which she had with her in the house, and she put them on Jacob her younger son.
She put the skins of the young goats on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.
Then she put the tasty dish and the bread that she had made into the hand of Jacob her son.
He went to his father and said, "My father." And he replied, "Here I am. Who are you, my son?"
Jacob said to his father, "I am Esau, your firstborn. I have done as you told me. Please sit up and eat some of my game, so that your heart may bless me."
Isaac asked his son, "How were you able to find it so quickly, my son?" He answered, "Because the LORD your God brought it to me."
Isaac said to Jacob, "My son, please come near so I can feel you, to see whether you are really my son Esau or not."
Jacob came near to Isaac his father, and Isaac felt him and said, "The voice is the voice of Jacob, but the hands are the hands of Esau."
Isaac did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like the hands of Esau his brother; so he blessed him.
He asked, "Are you really my son Esau?" Jacob answered, "Yes, I am."
Isaac said, "Bring it to me and let me eat of my son's game, so that my heart may bless you." Jacob brought it to him and he ate, then Jacob brought him wine and he drank.
Then Isaac his father said to him, "My son, come near and kiss me."
Jacob came near and kissed his father. Isaac smelled his clothing and blessed him, saying, "Ah, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field that the LORD has blessed.
May God give you the dew of heaven and the richness of the earth, an abundance of grain and new wine.

May the peoples serve you and the nations bow down to you. Be the lord over your brothers, and may the sons of your mother bow down to you. Whoever curses you shall be cursed, and whoever blesses you shall be blessed."
As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had just gone out from the presence of his father, Esau his brother returned from the hunt.
He too prepared a tasty dish and brought it to his father and said to him, "My father, please sit up and eat of your son's game, so that your heart may bless me."
Isaac his father asked him, "Who are you?" He answered, "I am Esau, your firstborn son."
Isaac trembled greatly and said, "Then who was it that hunted game and brought it to me? Before you came, I ate from all of it and blessed him — and indeed, he shall be blessed!"
When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried out a great and exceedingly bitter cry and said to his father, "Bless me too, O my father!"
Isaac answered, "Your brother came with deceit and took your blessing."

Esau said, "Is it not because his name was called 'Jacob'? He has supplanted me twice now: he took my birthright, and behold, now he has also taken my blessing." Then he asked, "Have you not reserved a blessing for me?"

Isaac answered Esau, "Behold, I have made him lord over you, and all his brothers I have given to him as servants, and I have sustained him with grain and new wine. Now what can I do for you, my son?"
Esau said to his father, "Have you only one blessing, my father? Bless me too, O my father!" Then Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
Isaac his father answered, "Behold, your dwelling shall be away from the richness of the earth and away from the dew of heaven above.
By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother. But when you become restless, you shall break his yoke from off your neck."
Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing his father had given him. And Esau said in his heart, "The days of mourning for my father are drawing near, and then I will kill Jacob my brother."
When the words of Esau her older son were reported to Rebekah, she sent for Jacob her younger son and said to him, "Look, Esau your brother is consoling himself by planning to kill you.
Now then, my son, listen to my voice. Rise and flee to Laban my brother in Haran.
Stay with him for a while, until your brother's fury subsides,
until your brother's anger turns away from you and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send and bring you back from there. Why should I lose both of you in one day?"

Then Rebekah said to Isaac, "I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth. If Jacob takes a wife from the daughters of Heth like these, from the daughters of this land, what good is my life?"
